web_results.title: 2026 Iranian Supreme Leader election - Wikipedia
web_results.description: An election for the third supreme leader of Iran will take place at an unspecified date in 2026 after the assassination of Ali Khamenei on 28 February 2026 during the Israeli–United States strikes on Iran.

web_results.title: The rise and fall of Iran’s ruthless and pragmatic Ayatollah Ali Khamenei | Ayatollah Ali Khamenei | The Guardian
web_results.description: The radical cleric took over as supreme leader in 1989 and is likely to be replaced by hardline figures

web_results.title: Will Khamenei leave Iran by...? Predictions & Odds | Polymarket
web_results.description: Propose resolutionThis market will resolve to "Yes" if it is confirmed that Ali Khamenei has left Iran for any length of time by February 28, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

web_results.title: Feb. 28: Hailing death of 'evil' Khamenei, Trump says strikes on Iran to persist until peace secured | The Times of Israel
web_results.description: Iran's former crown prince and now key opposition figure Reza Pahlavi attends a discussion during the 62nd Munich Security Conference (MSC) on February 13, 2026 in Munich, southern Germany. (Alexandra BEIER / AFP) Reza Pahlavi, the exiled son of the last Iranian Shah who was ousted in 1979, celebrates the apparent death of Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in US-Israeli strikes, predicts that the Islamic Republic will “very soon be consigned to the dustbin of history,” and adds that any attempt to tap a successor “is doomed to fail from the outset.”

news_results.title: Live: Iran confirms supreme leader Khamenei is dead after US, Israeli attacks | Reuters
news_results.description: The United States and Israel launched the most ambitious attack on Iran in decades on Saturday, and Iranian state media has confirmed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei had been killed.

news_results.description: A woman holds an illustration depicting Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as others wave Iranian national flags during a demonstration in support of the government and against US and Israeli strikes outside a mosque in Tehran on February 28, 2026.
